prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for acrolein (C₃H₄O) were computed using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, tier 'standard') in the gas phase. The calculation evaluated the neutral ground state, cation radical (+1, doublet), and anion radical (−1, doublet) to derive Fukui indices via Mulliken charge differences. All three electronic states converged. The most electrophilic site is the terminal vinyl carbon (C₄, f⁺ = 0.6423), and the most nucleophilic site is the carbonyl oxygen (O₁, f⁻ = 0.3939). The dual descriptor pattern identifies C₄ and C₃ as sites preferentially activated for nucleophilic attack, consistent with acrolein's character as a Michael acceptor. Charge conservation checks (sum(f⁺) and sum(f⁻) both = 1.0000) confirm SCF convergence for all states. Visualization output saved to the benchmark directory.</prose>
